title: Calibration
text: First, run model generate data fit . Next, define function calculate summary statistics model output. Specifically, calculate age-specific peak time incidence infections, amplitude incidence (difference maximum minimum incidence) total yearly incidence. RSVsim built functions calculate metrics model output. define function summary_fun combine metrics. use target ABC-rejection algorithm. must also specify function randomly samples prior distributions fitted parameters returns vector parameters. use latin hypercube sampling efficiently sample prior space. example fit b0, b1 phi parameters. vector parameter names fitted order prior samples must also provided set parameters run model.
code: # specify age categories to run the model with age_limits <- c(seq(0, 2, 0.2), seq(10, 60, 5))  # get contact matrix contact_population_list <- RSVsim_contact_matrix(country = "United Kingdom", age_limits = age_limits)  # get the default model parameters with a b0 value of 0.08, b1 value of 0, phi value of 0 and change the initial conditions  overrides <- list("b0" = 0.11, "b1" = 0.3, "phi" = 10)  parameters <- fixed_parameter_list <- RSVsim_parameters(overrides = overrides,                                                          contact_population_list = contact_population_list)  out <- RSVsim_run_model(parameters = parameters,                         times = seq(0, 365 * 2, 0.25), # maximum time to run the model for                         cohort_step_size = 0.2 * 365, # time at which to age people\                         warm_up = 365)  fitted_parameter_names <- c("b0", "b1", "phi")  nAges <- parameters[["nAges"]] ######################### ##### ABC functions ##### #########################  # calculate the summary statistics summary_fun <- function(out){   return(c(RSVsim_total_incidence(out), RSVsim_amplitude(out), RSVsim_peak(out))) }  target <- summary_fun(out)  # a function that samples from the priors is required # we use latin hypercube sampling to efficiently sample from the prior distributions prior_fun <- function(n_prior_attempts){      x <- lhs::randomLHS(n_prior_attempts, 3)      # adjusting the prior distributions   x[,1] <- qunif(x[,1], min = 0.05, max = 0.25)   x[,2] <- qunif(x[,2], min = 0, max = 1) # not necessary but added for completeness   x[,3] <- qunif(x[,3], min = -365.25/2, max = 365.25/2)      return(as.matrix(x, nrow = n_prior_attempts)) }  # a function that calculates the distance between the summary statistics is required dist_fun <- function(target, target_star, n = nAges){   return(     c(       RSVsim_abs_dist_fun(target[1:n], target_star[1:n]),       RSVsim_abs_dist_fun(target[(n+1):(n*2)], target_star[(n+1):(n*2)]),       RSVsim_shortest_periodic_dist_fun(target[(2*n+1):(n*3)], target_star[(2*n+1):(n*3)], period = 365.25)     )   )  }

title: Calibration
text: implement ABC-rejection algorithm must specify tolerance. specify specific tolerance metric. calculate tolerance values means approximately 0.1% simulations accepted run model 1000 times different parameter combinations drawn priors. calculate error (distances) summary statistics simulation target summary statistics. set range tolerances using different percentiles distances check number simulations accepted tolerance, use smallest tolerance least 1 simulation accepted. method calculating suitable tolerance values optional custom epsilon values can specified user. run model specify number particles fit, seeds particle. number cores can also specified - greater one parallel processing implemented using parallel socket cluster parallel R package.
code: ################################# ##### setting the tolerance ##### #################################  # this code is optional but is used to approximate epsilon values that will give us an acceptance rate of 0.1%  # calculating a tolerances that means at least 1 particle combination is accepted every 100 simulations # getting 1000 samples from the priors set.seed(123) n_check <- 1000 prior_params <- prior_fun(n_check)  # simulating the summary statistics for each particle combination prior_distances <- sapply(1:n_check, function(i){   parameters_in <- RSVsim_update_parameters(fixed_parameter_list, fitted_parameter_names, prior_params[i, ])      out <- RSVsim_run_model(parameters = parameters_in,                    times = seq(0, 365*2, 0.25), # maximum time to run the model for                    cohort_step_size = 0.2*365, # time at which to age people                    warm_up = 365)      return(dist_fun(target,                    summary_fun(out)                   )          ) })  # calculating the number of particles for which all summary statistics are within the tolerance given different percentiles of the summary statistics for all the particles  # selecting the tolerance with at least 1 model simulation using the prior samples that is within the tolerance  nsuccess <- rep(NA, n_check) q <- seq(0.01, 1, 0.01)  for(i in 1:100){   epsilon_check <- round(apply(prior_distances, 1, quantile, probs = c(q[i])), digits = 2)   nsuccess[i] <- sum(sapply(1:100, function(j){all(prior_distances[,j] <= epsilon_check)})) }  # selecting a tolerance where at least 1 particle is accepted for the 1000 simulations epsilon <- round(apply(prior_distances, 1, quantile, probs = c(q[min(which(nsuccess >= 1))])), digits = 2) ############################################### ##### running the ABC-rejection algorithm ##### ###############################################  nparticles = 500  used_seeds_all <- seq(1, nparticles)  ncores <- 8  fit <- RSVsim_ABC_rejection(target = target,                             epsilon = epsilon,                             summary_fun = summary_fun,                             dist_fun = dist_fun,                             prior_fun = prior_fun,                             n_prior_attempts = 10000,                             nparticles = nparticles,                             used_seeds_all = used_seeds_all,                             ncores = ncores,                             fitted_parameter_names = fitted_parameter_names,                             fixed_parameter_list = fixed_parameter_list,                             times = seq(0, 365, 0.25), # maximum time to run the model for                             cohort_step_size = 0.2*365, # time at which to age people\                             warm_up = NULL)  # to visualise the posterior distributions fit_b0 <- unlist(fit[,"b0"]) fit_b1 <- unlist(fit[,"b1"]) fit_phi <- unlist(fit[,"phi"])  hist(fit_b0) hist(fit_b1) hist(fit_phi)

title: Calibration
text: also provide function fit model using ABC-SMC. ABC-SMC algorithm requires function return prior density sequential reduction tolerances. Similar ABC-rejection algorithm, use previously simulated summary statistics calculate tolerances corresponding given number particle combinations accepted. decreasing acceptance rate; tolerances stored matrix column corresponding summary statistic rows corresponding tolerance level (lowest tolerance last row) (G; generation). , method calculating suitable tolerance values optional custom epsilon_matrix values can specified user. can run ABC-SMC using code . function also requires: (1) matrix random seeds generation accepted particle (used_seed_matrix), (2) minimum maximum values fitted parameter used prior distributions (particle_low particle_up respectively), (3) number accepted particles generation (nparticles), (4) number prior samples try accepted particle combination (n_prior_attempts), (5) names fitted parameters (fitted_parameter_names, must characters can include indexing parameters stored vectors matrices), (6) list parameters equivalent used RSVsim_run_model parameters (fixed_parameter_list) (7) times cohort step sizes used RSVsim_run_model. Running model fitted parameters. ABC algorithms return matrices fitted parameter values. RSVsim_ABC_rejection main output RSVsim_ABC_SMC list fitted parameter values returned generation. fitted parameter columns correspond parameter rows correspond accepted parameter combination. run model one particle (combination fitted parameters) can therefore select correct matrix fitted parameters use RSVsim_update_parameters adjust parameters. example, want run first accepted particle:
code: # function that returns the prior likelihood of each parameter (prior density function) # returns a vector of the same length as the number of parameters prior_dens_fun <- function(x){      # adjusting the prior distributions   return(c(dunif(x[1], min = 0.05, max = 0.25),            dunif(x[2], min = 0, max = 1),            dunif(x[3], min = -365.25/2, max = 365.25/2)            )          ) } nsuccess <- rep(NA, n_check) q <- seq(1/n_check, 1, 1/n_check) for(i in 1:n_check){   epsilon_check <- round(apply(prior_distances, 1, quantile, probs = c(q[i])), digits = 5)   nsuccess[i] <- sum(sapply(1:n_check, function(j){all(prior_distances[,j] <= epsilon_check)})) }  acceptance_rate <- sort(c(1, 10, 50, 75), decreasing = TRUE)  q_percentile <- vector(mode = "list", length = length(acceptance_rate))  q_percentile <- lapply(acceptance_rate, function(ar){q[min(which(nsuccess/n_check * 100 > ar))]}) # 1  q_percentile_ar1 <- q_percentile[[which(acceptance_rate == 1)]]  # selecting a tolerance where at least 1 particle is accepted for the 1000 simulations epsilon_matrix <- as.matrix(do.call(rbind, lapply(q_percentile, function(q){round(apply(prior_distances, 1, quantile, probs = q), digits = 3)})))  G <- nrow(epsilon_matrix) used_seed_matrix <- matrix(seq(1, nparticles * G), nrow = G)  fit_smc <- RSVsim_ABC_SMC(target = target,                           epsilon_matrix = epsilon_matrix,                           summary_fun = summary_fun,                           dist_fun = dist_fun,                           prior_fun = prior_fun,                           n_prior_attempts = 10000,                           used_seed_matrix = used_seed_matrix,                           prior_dens_fun = prior_dens_fun,                           particle_low = c(0.05, 0, -365.25/2),                           particle_up = c(0.25, 1, 365.25/2),                           nparticles = nparticles,                           ncores = ncores,                           fitted_parameter_names = fitted_parameter_names,                           fixed_parameter_list = fixed_parameter_list,                           times = seq(0, 365, 0.25), # maximum time to run the model for                           cohort_step_size = 0.2*365, # time at which to age people                           warm_up = NULL) # ABC rejection parameters_ABC_rejection <- RSVsim_update_parameters(parameters, fitted_parameter_names, c(fit[1, c("b0", "b1", "phi")]))  # ABC SMC # for the ABC SMC algorithm I must select the fitted parameters from the last generation  fit_params_SMC <- fit_smc$fitted_parameters[[G]] colnames(fit_params_SMC) <- fitted_parameter_names  parameters_ABC_SMC <- RSVsim_update_parameters(parameters, fitted_parameter_names, c(fit_params_SMC[1, c("b0", "b1", "phi")]))

title: Hospitalisations
text: Hospitalisation rates (among whole population) might sampled total incidence known. , can use RSVsim estimate incidence setting age specific hospitalisation rates estimated use incidence hospitalisation rates calculate probability hospitalisation given infection (infection hospitalisation rate; IHR). probability hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection age group ii calculated p(hosp|inf,)=HiIip(hosp|inf,) = \frac{H_{}}{I_{}}, HiH_{} number hospitalisations age group ii IiI_{} corresponding incidence. age-specific hospitalisation rates per person hi=HiNih_{}=\frac{H_{}}{N_{}}, NiN_{} population size, meaning p(hosp|inf)=hiIi/Nip(hosp|inf) = \frac{h_{}}{I_{}/N_{}}. Similarly, can calculate p(hosp|inf)p(hosp|inf) unvaccinated people assuming given vaccine efficacy hospitalisation conditional infection vaccinated state (VEvCVE^{C}_{v}) hospitalisation rates, thus, p(hosp|inf,)=hiI1iN1i+∑2VIviNvi(1−VEviC) p(hosp|inf,) = \frac{h_{}}{\frac{I_{1i}}{N_{1i}} + \sum_{2}^{V} \frac{I_{vi}}{N_{vi}}(1 - VE^{C}_{vi})} overall vaccine efficacy hospitalisation (VEHVE_{H}) vaccine efficacy infection (VEIVE_{}) can calculate vaccine efficacy hospitalisation conditional infection thus, VEiC=1−1−VEiH1−VEiI VE_{}^{C} = 1 - \frac{1 - VE_{}^{H}}{1 - VE_{}^{}}  provide RSVsim_VE_hosp_given_inf function calculate ; VEIVE^{} ages VEHVE^{H}. example assume existing vaccination. default vaccination states 1 (unvaccinated) 2 (vaccinated). First, use RSVsim_run_model approximate incidence. can use RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf function estimate p(hosp|inf)p(hosp|inf) given values hih_{} (note must per person). must also provide times hospitalisations estimated vaccine efficacy hospitalisation vaccinated state.
code: times <- seq(0, 365.25*11, 0.25) warm_up <- 365.25 * 10 cohort_step_size <- 1/12 * 365.25  age_limits <- c(seq(0, 5, 1/12), seq(10, 75, 5))  contact_population_list <- RSVsim_contact_matrix(age_limits = age_limits)  parameters <- RSVsim_parameters(contact_population_list = contact_population_list)  sim_status_quo <- RSVsim_run_model(parameters = parameters,                                    times = times,                                    cohort_step_size = cohort_step_size,                                    warm_up = warm_up) # age specific number of hospitalisations per person  hosp_rate <- c(5338.91375, 1751.145228, 715.9402014, 208.4621667, 10.86702733, 2.58129891, 2.321354938, 1.868628613,                  1.320440418, 1.834551244, 1.883359955, 2.861263391, 4.744902927, 6.645799647, 9.348531561, 14.3538506,                  22.62406397, 32.94296492, 87.63366855) / 100000  age_limits_hosp_rate <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55, 60, 65, 70, 75) # lower age limits of the hospitalisation rate data  # vaccine efficacy against hospitalisation conditional on infection VE_hosp <- matrix(rep(0.8, length(age_limits)), ncol = 1) # age-specific vaccine efficacy against hospitalisation VE_inf <- parameters$VE_inf VE_hosp_given_inf <- RSVsim_VE_hosp_given_inf(VE_hosp = VE_hosp, VE_inf = VE_inf)  prob_hosp_given_inf_list <- RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf(hosp_rate = hosp_rate,                                                        age_limits_hosp_rate = age_limits_hosp_rate,                                                        hosp_rate_min_time = 0, # time frame over which to sum incidence                                                        hosp_rate_max_time = 365.25,                                                        sim_status_quo = sim_status_quo,                                                        VE_hosp_given_inf = VE_hosp_given_inf,                                                        VE_hosp_vacc_states = c(2) # vector identifying the vaccination states that are vaccinated (position corresponds to the column position in VE_hosp)                                                        )

title: Hospitalisations
text: also provide function estimate number hospitalisations different scenarios RSVsim_run_model outputs known p(hosp|inf)p(hosp|inf). First, simulate novel scenario use RSVsim_hospitalisations function estimate number hospitalisations time-step. example, use p(hosp|inf)p(hosp|inf) values estimated using RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf function. RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf function returns list p(hosp|inf,)p(hosp|inf,) sim_status_quo model outputs hospitalisations.
code: ################################# ##### transmission dynamics ##### #################################  # single 30 day vaccination campaign vaccine_times <- c(0, c(90, 120) + 365.25 * 10) vaccine_period <- diff(c(vaccine_times, max(vaccine_times))) vaccine_period[vaccine_period == 0] <- 30 nVaccTimes <- length(vaccine_times)  nAges <- length(contact_population_list$age_limits)  # vaccination campaign target coverage: 80% in the oldest age group vaccine_cov <- rbind(matrix(rep(0, (nAges-1) * nVaccTimes), nrow = (nAges-1), ncol = nVaccTimes),                      c(0, 0.8, 0))  nVaccStates <- 2 VE_inf <- matrix(rep(0.5, nAges * (nVaccStates-1)), nrow = nAges) # vaccine efficacy against infection gamma_vaccine <- 1 / (365.25 * 2)  vac_parameters <- RSVsim_parameters(overrides = list("vaccine_times" = vaccine_times,                                                      "vaccine_period" = vaccine_period,                                                      "nVaccTimes" = nVaccTimes,                                                      "vaccine_cov" = vaccine_cov,                                                      "nVaccStates" = nVaccStates,                                                      "VE_inf" = VE_inf,                                                      "gamma_vaccine" = gamma_vaccine),                                     contact_population_list = contact_population_list)  sim_vaccination <- RSVsim_run_model(parameters = vac_parameters,                                     times = times,                                     cohort_step_size = cohort_step_size, # time at which to age people                                     warm_up = warm_up)  ############################ ##### hospitalisations ##### ############################  sim_vaccination <- RSVsim_hospitalisations(prob_hosp_given_inf = prob_hosp_given_inf_list[["prob_hosp_given_inf_uv"]],                                            age_limits_hosp_rate = age_limits_hosp_rate,                                            sim_scenario = sim_vaccination,                                            VE_hosp_given_inf = VE_hosp_given_inf,                                            VE_hosp_vacc_states = c(2))

title: Introduction
text: R/RSVsim_contact_matrix.R function obtains contact matrices age groupings less one year. , socialmixr (https://epiforecasts.io/socialmixr/) package used obtain contact matrix age groupings nearest whole year, using POLYMOD data (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pmed.0050074). contact matrix calculated total contacts population symmetric (assuming population data World Population Prospects United Nations). Within age groups less one year, contacts divided evenly according size age group (years). function takes two arguments: (1) country obtain POLYMOD contact data (2) lower age limits age groups.
code: # specifying the some age limits to use with the model age_limits <- c(seq(0, 2, 0.2), seq(10, 60, 20))  contact_population_list <- RSVsim_contact_matrix(country = "United Kingdom", age_limits = age_limits)

title: Introduction
text: obtain list default parameters run model use R/RSVsim_parameters.R function. parameters can changed using overrides argument; example override b0b0 (transmission rate coefficient) value 0.150.15. “fitted” argument refers parameters excluded - function return parameters named vector. Initial conditions defined parameter list. initial conditions given, assumed 0.1% prevalence primary infection compartment across ages everyone else susceptible primary infection. new-borns assumed susceptible primary infection compartment.
code: parameters <- RSVsim_parameters(overrides = list("b0" = 0.15, "b1" = 1, "phi" = 0),                                  contact_population_list = contact_population_list)

title: Introduction
text: run model R/RSVsim_run_model.R function used. Cohort aging used age population. time, proportion cohort aged, proportion determined length time people aged (cohort_step_size) proportion total length time age group. simulated number people susceptible infected, whether infection primary secondary tracked model outputs. incidence detected incidence since previous time-step calculated provided model outputs. calculate detected incidence age-specific detection probabilities used, default parameters assume people (1%) age 2 years old tested. age-specific prevalence also calculated. incidence doses given number occurred time step. time-steps cohort ageing occurs included simulation. Usually difference time-steps provided times different meaning incidence doses consistently calculated time frame can therefore appear rapidly change. estimate incidence doses consistent time-steps must therefore sum incidence grouped primary simulation times.
code: times <- seq(0, 365 * 10, 0.25)  out <- RSVsim_run_model(parameters = parameters,                         times = times, # maximum time to run the model for                         cohort_step_size = 1/12 * 365, # time at which to age people\                         warm_up = NULL) # times are incremented by 0.25 when running the model in this example  out |> dplyr::mutate(time_round = round(time / 0.25) * 0.25) |>    dplyr::group_by(time_round, vacc_state, age, age_chr) |>    dplyr::summarise(Incidence = sum(Incidence), doses = sum(doses))

title: RSV Transmission Model
text: deterministic, ODE model Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) transmission. package constant development, change without notice. current model outputs yet fully tested.

title: RSV Transmission Model
text: can install development version RSVsim GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("IsaacStopard/RSVsim")

title: RSV Transmission Model
text: model adapted version susceptible-exposed-infectious-recovered-susceptible (SEIRS) deterministic, ordinary differential equation (ODE) model outlined : Hogan, . B., Campbell, P. T., Blyth, C. C., Lim, F. J., Fathima, P., Davis, S., … & Glass, K. (2017). Potential impact maternal vaccine RSV: mathematical modelling study. Vaccine, 35(45), 6172-6179, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.vaccine.2017.09.043, Nazareno, . L., Newall, . T., Muscatello, D. J., Hogan, . B., & Wood, J. G. (2024). Modelling epidemiological impact maternal respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccination Australia. Vaccine, 42(26), 126418, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.vaccine.2024.126418. brief, model age-structured primary (first, pp) secondary infections (subsequent infection, ss) specifically tracked. force infection determined seasonal forcing function (β\beta) age-specific contact matrix (mean contacts per person, MM). population stratified susceptible (SS), exposed (EE), infectious (II) recovered states (RR).

title: Function to run an Approximate Bayesian Computation Sequential Monte Carlo (ABC-SMC) algorithm — RSVsim_ABC_SMC
text: Runs ABC-SMC algorithm.

title: Function to run an Approximate Bayesian Computation Sequential Monte Carlo (ABC-SMC) algorithm — RSVsim_ABC_SMC
code: RSVsim_ABC_SMC(   target,   epsilon_matrix,   summary_fun,   dist_fun,   prior_fun,   n_prior_attempts,   used_seed_matrix,   prior_dens_fun,   particle_low,   particle_up,   nparticles,   ncores = 1,   fitted_parameter_names,   fixed_parameter_list,   times = seq(0, 365.25 * 5, 0.25),   cohort_step_size = 1/12 * 365.25,   warm_up = 365.25 * 4 )

title: Function to run an Approximate Bayesian Computation Sequential Monte Carlo (ABC-SMC) algorithm — RSVsim_ABC_SMC
text: target Values fit . epsilon_matrix Matrix tolerance values. Different columns correspond values different data points different rows correspond values different generations. summary_fun Function calculate summary statistics equivalent target values. dist_fun Function calculate error target summary_fun outputs. prior_fun Function sample priors parameters. Must return vector. n_prior_attempts Number samples try accepted particle. used_seed_matrix Matrix seeds: number rows must equal number generations number columns must equal nparticles. prior_dens_fun Function calculates probability density parameters given prior distributions. joint probability product values returned function. Note argument prior_dens_fun vector length number fitted parameters. particle_low Lower bounds parameters. particle_up Upper bounds parameters. nparticles Integer. Number samples approximate posterior. ncores Number cores. greater one run parallel. fitted_parameter_names Vector names parameters estimated. fit vectors matrices individual element must identified fitted_parameter_names. fixed_parameter_list List original parameter values run model. times Simulation times. Default: 0 365.25 days intervals 0.25 days. minimum interval size 1E-5. cohort_step_size Time steps run model adjusting ages cohorts. Default: 1 month. .numeric(cohort_step_size) == FALSE cohort ageing applied. Can maximum 7 decimal places. warm_up Length time-points exclude calculating likelihood. Default: NULL.

title: Function to run an Approximate Bayesian Computation Sequential Monte Carlo (ABC-SMC) algorithm — RSVsim_ABC_SMC
text: List fixed parameters, max_t warm_up.

code: RSVsim_ABC_rejection(   target,   epsilon,   summary_fun,   dist_fun,   prior_fun,   n_prior_attempts,   nparticles,   used_seeds_all,   ncores = 1,   fitted_parameter_names,   fixed_parameter_list,   times = seq(0, 365.25 * 5, 0.25),   cohort_step_size = 1/12 * 365.25,   warm_up = 365.25 * 4 )

title: Function to run an Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC) rejection algorithm — RSVsim_ABC_rejection
text: target Values fit . epsilon Acceptable error target. summary_fun Function calculate summary statistics equivalent target values. dist_fun Function calculate error target summary_fun outputs. prior_fun Function sample priors parameters. Must return vector. n_prior_attempts Number random samples prior attempt accepted particle. nparticles Integer. Number samples approximate posterior. used_seeds_all Vector. Seeds used generating prior samples accepted particle. ncores Number cores. greater one run parallel. fitted_parameter_names Vector names parameters estimated. fit vectors matrices individual element must identified fitted_parameter_names. fixed_parameter_list List original parameter values run model. times Simulation times. Default: 0 365.25 days intervals 0.25 days. minimum interval size 1E-5. cohort_step_size Time steps run model adjusting ages cohorts. Default: 1 month. .numeric(cohort_step_size) == FALSE cohort ageing applied. Can maximum 7 decimal places. warm_up Length time-points exclude calculating likelihood. Default: NULL.

title: Create contact matrix for RSVsim — RSVsim_contact_matrix
text: country Country use contact_matrix function socialmixr package. Can given country name 2 digit ISO code. United Kingdom default. age_limits Lower limits age groups run simulation. Ages must years. Note smallest age limits allowed 1E-8.

title: Create contact matrix for RSVsim — RSVsim_contact_matrix
text: List including contact matrix (matrix_mean mean per person, matrix_contacts total contacts), age limits, age distribution used calculate total contacts maximum age contact data.

what: Calculate number of hospitalisations for RSVsim_run_model output — RSVsim_hospitalisations
title: Calculate number of hospitalisations for RSVsim_run_model output — RSVsim_hospitalisations
text: Function estimate number hospitalisations modelled incidence probability hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection.

title: Calculate number of hospitalisations for RSVsim_run_model output — RSVsim_hospitalisations
code: RSVsim_hospitalisations(   prob_hosp_given_inf,   age_limits_hosp_rate,   sim_scenario,   VE_hosp_given_inf,   VE_hosp_vacc_states )

title: Calculate number of hospitalisations for RSVsim_run_model output — RSVsim_hospitalisations
text: prob_hosp_given_inf Age-specific probability hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection. Ages must correspond age_limits_hosp_rate. age_limits_hosp_rate Vector ages corresponding first age age category (1) hospitalisation rates (hosp_rate) (2) probability hospitalisation given infection (prob_hosp_given_inf). ages must present age_limits used run simulations. sim_scenario RSVsim_run_model output. Hospitalisations calculated scenario using estimated probability hospitalisation given infection. VE_hosp_given_inf Matrix age-specific vaccine efficacy hospitalisation given infection (VE_hosp_given_inf). Rows must correspond ages age_limits, columns must correspond vaccinated states used RSVsim_run_model. VE_hosp_vacc_states vector integers indicating vaccinated states used RSVsim_run_model.

title: Calculate number of hospitalisations for RSVsim_run_model output — RSVsim_hospitalisations
text: sim_scenario dataframe hospitalisations column

title: Get parameters for RSVsim — RSVsim_parameters
text: Provides default parameters run RSV model.

title: Get parameters for RSVsim — RSVsim_parameters
code: RSVsim_parameters(overrides = list(), contact_population_list)

title: Get parameters for RSVsim — RSVsim_parameters
text: overrides List default parameters change. contact_population_list List outputs create_contact_matrix function.

title: Get parameters for RSVsim — RSVsim_parameters
text: Parameter list. Default values: b0: 0.125. Transmission rate coefficient. b1: 0.1. Amplitude seasonal forcing transmission rate. phi: 0. Phase shift seasonal forcing. delta: 1/4. Inverse latent period. gamma_s: 1/8. Inverse infectious period subsequent (secondary) infections. gamma_p: 1/9. Inverse infectious period first (primary) infections. nu: 1/200. Inverse duration natural immunity. omega_vect: 0.35 greater 5 years old else 1. Reduced infectiousness due immunity early life. prop_detected_vect: age-specific proportion detected. sigma_vect: 0.7 1 months old, 0.8 2 months old, 0.9 3 months old, else 1. Reduced susceptibility due immunity early life. alpha_vect = 0.4 less 10 years old else 0.3. Reduced susceptibility secondary infections age group. nAges: must obtained contact_population_list. Number age groups - length age_limits. age_limits: must obtained contact_population_list. Lower age limits years. matrix_per_person: must obtained contact_population_list. Contact matrix mean contacts per person. max_age: must obtained contact_population_list. Maximum age years. age_chr: Age limits character vector. size_cohorts: differences ages days - used calculate size cohorts. rel_sizes: relative size cohort (age category duration days divided total number days). total_population: 1861923. Total population size. nVaccStates: 2. Number vaccination states. Default: 1: unvaccinated, 2: vaccinated. Minimum 2. gamma_vaccine: 1 / (365*2). Rate waning vaccinated states. Size vector must nVaccStates minus one. nVaccTimes: 5. Length start times vaccination distributions. vaccine_times: c(0, 365.25, 365.25 + 30, 730.5, 730.5 + 30). Times vaccination distributions. First time 0. vaccine_period: rep(30, 5). Duration vaccination distributions. vaccine_cov: matrix rows corresponding vaccine_times columns corresponding ages.  Age-specific proportion unvaccinated people vaccinated end vaccination distribution vaccine_time, assuming waning vaccination. vaccination rate calculated -log(1 - vaccine_cov) / vaccine_period. Default 0 coverage ages. Changes effective coverage given model output.  VE_inf: 0.5 ages vaccinated states. Vaccine efficacy infection age group vaccinated state. Number rows must equal number age groups, number columns equal nVaccStates - 1. Sp0, Ss0, Ep0, Es0, Ip0, Is0, R0, Incidence0, doses0: initial conditions run model compartment - given prevalence initial conditions calculated function. List. Default: NULL. NULL: 0.1% RSV prevalence assumed people primary infection, seeded beginning simulation. people assumed susceptible primary infection.

what: Probability of hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection — RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf
title: Probability of hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection — RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf
text: Calculates age-specific probability hospitalisation given infection, using observed hospitalisation rates per person among population whole RSVsim estimate number infections per person.

title: Probability of hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection — RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf
code: RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf(   hosp_rate,   age_limits_hosp_rate,   hosp_rate_min_time,   hosp_rate_max_time,   sim_status_quo,   VE_hosp_given_inf,   VE_hosp_vacc_states )

title: Probability of hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection — RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf
text: hosp_rate Vector age-specific RSV hospitalisation rates (per person) status quo scenario. age_limits_hosp_rate Vector ages corresponding first age age category (1) hospitalisation rates (hosp_rate) (2) probability hospitalisation given infection (prob_hosp_given_inf). ages must present age_limits used run simulations. hosp_rate_min_time Minimum simulation time select summing hospitalisations calculate hospitalisation rates. hosp_rate_max_time Maximum simulation time select summing hospitalisations calculate hospitalisation rates. sim_status_quo RSVsim_run_model output used estimate number infections calculating probability hospitalisation given infection. VE_hosp_given_inf Matrix age-specific vaccine efficacy hospitalisation given infection (VE_hosp_given_inf). Rows must correspond ages age_limits, columns must correspond vaccinated states used RSVsim_run_model. VE_hosp_vacc_states vector integers indicating vaccinated states used RSVsim_run_model.

title: Probability of hospitalisation given unvaccinated infection — RSVsim_prob_hosp_given_inf
text: List probability hospitalisation given infection  sim_status_quo model outputs hospitalisations.

what: Runs the RSV model using odin and dust — RSVsim_run_model
title: Runs the RSV model using odin and dust — RSVsim_run_model
text: Function run transmission model cohort aging. Prevalence, incidence given time-steps incidence rate per day also calculated.

title: Runs the RSV model using odin and dust — RSVsim_run_model
code: RSVsim_run_model(   parameters,   times = seq(0, 365.25 * 1, 0.25),   cohort_step_size = 1/12 * 365.25,   warm_up = NULL )

title: Runs the RSV model using odin and dust — RSVsim_run_model
text: parameters List parameters RSVsim_parameters function. times Simulation times. Default: 0 365.25 days intervals 0.25 days. minimum interval size 1E-5. cohort_step_size Time steps run model adjusting ages cohorts. Default: 1 month. .numeric(cohort_step_size) == FALSE cohort ageing applied. Can maximum 7 decimal places. warm_up Length time-points exclude calculating likelihood. Default: NULL.

title: Runs the RSV model using odin and dust — RSVsim_run_model
text: Simulation output (dataframe). dataframe, age refers lowest age age group.
